about.title: About ToolsBox
about.subtitle: All-in-One Online Tool Collection
about.description: ToolsBox is a pure front-end online tool collection covering image processing, text editing, format conversion, development assistance, office productivity, and more. All tools run locally in your browser — no data is uploaded to any server, ensuring your privacy.
about.feature1Desc: All tools use browser native APIs — no backend server, no software installation required. Data is processed entirely on your device and never uploaded.
about.feature2Desc: Responsive design works perfectly on desktop, tablet, and mobile. Open your browser anytime, anywhere — no device or platform restrictions.
about.feature3Desc: Full support for both Chinese and English interfaces. Switch languages with one click and all content updates instantly.
about.feature4Desc: Supports dark and light themes to protect your eyes while keeping the interface beautiful. Theme preference is persisted locally.
about.privacyDesc: Your data security is our top priority. All tools in ToolsBox run locally in your browser. We have no backend servers and do not collect, store, or transmit any of your data. Files you process, text you enter, and outputs you generate exist only on your device. All temporary data is automatically cleared when you close the page.

imageTools.cropFeatureDesc: Our online image crop tool supports free cropping and aspect ratio locking to help you quickly extract the desired area from any image. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers, ensuring your privacy.
imageTools.cropFeature1Desc: Drag to select any area with precise control over the crop region
imageTools.cropFeature2Desc: Preset ratios like 1:1, 16:9, 4:3 for one-click locking
imageTools.cropFeature3Desc: Cropped images retain original resolution with no quality loss
imageTools.cropStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to crop
imageTools.cropStep2: Drag to adjust the crop area on the image, or select a preset ratio
imageTools.cropStep3: Confirm the crop area and click "Apply Crop"
imageTools.cropStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.cropFaq1Q: Will the cropped image be uploaded to a server?
imageTools.cropFaq1A: No. All cropping operations are performed locally in your browser using Canvas API. Your image data never leaves your device.
imageTools.cropFaq2A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.cropFaq3Q: Will the cropped image become blurry?
imageTools.cropFaq3A: No. Cropping only extracts a portion of the image without any scaling or compression, so the cropped result maintains the original sharpness.
imageTools.compressFeatureDesc: Our online image compression tool helps you significantly reduce image file sizes while maintaining visual quality. Supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP output formats with adjustable quality and max width — ideal for web uploads, email attachments, and more.
imageTools.compressFeature1Desc: Output in JPEG, PNG, or WebP to meet different needs
imageTools.compressFeature3Desc: Powered by Canvas API — pure browser processing, no upload wait
imageTools.compressStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to compress
imageTools.compressStep2: Adjust the quality slider, output format, and max width
imageTools.compressStep3: Click "Start Compress" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.compressStep4: View the compression comparison and click "Download" to save
imageTools.compressFaq1A: Compression does reduce quality to some degree, but by adjusting the quality parameter, you can find a good balance. We recommend starting at 80% quality.
imageTools.compressFaq2Q: Why does WebP format compress better?
imageTools.compressFaq2A: WebP is a modern image format by Google that uses more advanced compression algorithms. At the same quality level, WebP files are typically 25-35% smaller than JPEG, making it ideal for web use.
imageTools.compressFaq3Q: Will the compressed image resolution change?
imageTools.compressFaq3A: If you adjust the max width parameter, the image width will scale accordingly with height adjusted proportionally. If you keep max width unchanged, only quality reduction is applied to compress file size.
imageTools.resizeFeatureDesc: Our online image resize tool supports custom dimensions and preset ratios to help you quickly adjust image size. Supports aspect ratio lock to prevent image distortion. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.resizeFeature1Desc: High-quality resize algorithm powered by Canvas API, maintaining image clarity
imageTools.resizeFeature2Desc: Automatically locks aspect ratio to prevent image stretching
imageTools.resizeFeature3Desc: Provides common ratios like 50%, 75%, 150%, 200% for quick resize

imageTools.resizeFaq1Q: Will the resized image become blurry?
imageTools.resizeFaq1A: Resizing may cause some quality loss, especially when enlarging images. We recommend keeping the resize ratio within 200% for best visual results.
imageTools.resizeFaq2Q: How to keep the image from distorting?
imageTools.resizeFaq2A: Aspect ratio is locked by default. When you modify the width, the height automatically adjusts proportionally. To set a custom aspect ratio, unlock it first and set width and height separately.
imageTools.resizeFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.

imageTools.vintageFaq1Q: How does the vintage filter work?
imageTools.vintageFaq1A: The vintage filter uses a classic sepia tone algorithm that applies weighted calculations to RGB channels, simulating the warm tone of traditional silver halide photos for a nostalgic feel.
imageTools.vintageFaq2Q: Does vintage processing affect image quality?
imageTools.vintageFaq2A: Vintage processing only changes the color distribution, maintaining the original resolution and sharpness. However, color information is replaced and cannot be restored to the original.
imageTools.vintageFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.sharpenFeatureDesc: Our online sharpen tool enhances image detail clarity using a convolution kernel algorithm that highlights edge contours. Ideal for fixing mild blur and improving image texture quality.

imageTools.sharpenFaq1A: Over-sharpening can introduce noise and jagged edges, especially along object contours. We recommend keeping sharpen intensity under 150% for natural-looking results.
imageTools.sharpenFaq2Q: Can sharpening fix severely blurred images?
imageTools.sharpenFaq2A: Sharpening enhances existing detail contrast but cannot restore lost detail. For severely blurred images, the effect is limited. We recommend re-shooting or using professional restoration tools.
imageTools.sharpenFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.

imageTools.roundedFaq1Q: What color is the background outside the rounded area?
imageTools.roundedFaq1A: The area outside the rounded corners is transparent. The output format is PNG with alpha channel support. If you need a white background, add it in another tool after downloading.
imageTools.roundedFaq2Q: Does rounding affect the image content?
imageTools.roundedFaq2A: No. Rounding only crops the four corners of the image and does not affect the main content. The image resolution remains unchanged.
imageTools.roundedFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality and alpha channel support.

imageTools.watermarkFaq1A: Yes. Text and image watermarks can be used simultaneously. Text is overlaid on top of the image watermark. All parameters can be adjusted independently.
imageTools.watermarkFaq2Q: Does watermarking affect the original image?
imageTools.watermarkFaq2A: No. The watermark is composited on top of the original image to create a new image. The original file is never modified. The processed result contains the watermark.

imageTools.colorExtractFaq1Q: How many colors can be extracted?
imageTools.colorExtractFaq1A: Up to 8 dominant colors can be extracted. If the image has few colors, the count will be lower. Extracted colors are sorted by proportion from highest to lowest.
imageTools.colorExtractFaq2Q: How accurate is the color extraction?
imageTools.colorExtractFaq2A: The algorithm uses color quantization to merge similar colors and extract the most representative ones. For colorful images, the results accurately reflect the dominant color scheme.
imageTools.colorExtractFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF.

imageTools.base64Step4: Click Copy Base64 to save the result, or switch to Decode mode to restore the image
imageTools.base64Faq1Q: Can Base64 strings be used directly in web pages?
imageTools.base64Faq1A: Yes. The generated Base64 string is a complete data URL that can be used directly as an img tag src attribute or as a CSS background-image url value without extra network requests.
imageTools.base64Faq2Q: Does the file size increase after Base64 encoding?
imageTools.base64Faq2A: Yes. Base64 encoding converts binary data to text format, increasing data size by approximately 33%. This is fine for small images, but for large images, URL references are recommended over Base64 embedding.
imageTools.base64Faq3A: All common image formats are supported for input. Output format can be PNG, JPEG, or WebP to meet different needs.
imageTools.formatConvertFeatureDesc: Our online format converter helps you convert images between PNG, JPEG, WebP, and BMP formats. Supports quality adjustment for JPEG and WebP. All operations are performed locally in your browser.
imageTools.formatConvertFeature1Desc: Supports PNG, JPEG, WebP, and BMP output formats for common needs
imageTools.formatConvertFeature2Desc: JPEG and WebP support 10-100% quality adjustment for balancing quality and file size
imageTools.formatConvertFeature3Desc: Shows original vs. converted file size comparison for an intuitive understanding of compression
imageTools.formatConvertStep1: Click the upload button to select an image to convert
imageTools.formatConvertStep3: For JPEG or WebP, adjust the output quality
imageTools.formatConvertStep4: Click the Convert button, preview the result, and download
imageTools.formatConvertFaq1A: PNG and BMP are lossless formats with no quality loss. JPEG and WebP are lossy formats. Setting quality to 100% minimizes quality loss. Choose the appropriate format and quality based on your needs.
imageTools.formatConvertFaq2Q: Why does BMP output as PNG?
imageTools.formatConvertFaq2A: The browser Canvas API does not natively support BMP output. When BMP is selected, the actual output uses PNG format. For true BMP files, use professional image editing software.
imageTools.formatConvertFaq3A: All common image formats are supported for input, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF.
imageTools.compareFeatureDesc: Our online image comparison tool lets you visually compare original and processed effects. Supports side-by-side and slider comparison modes. Built-in grayscale and blur effects for testing. All operations are performed locally in your browser.

imageTools.compareFeature2Desc: Built-in grayscale and blur effects for quick comparison testing
imageTools.compareFeature3Desc: Drag the slider divider to see effect differences across different areas in real-time
imageTools.compareStep1: Click the upload button to select an image for comparison
imageTools.compareStep2: Select an effect (grayscale or blur) to generate the processed image
imageTools.compareStep3: Switch between side-by-side and slider comparison modes
imageTools.compareStep4: In slider mode, drag the divider to compare effects across different areas
imageTools.compareFaq1Q: Can I compare two different images?
imageTools.compareFaq1A: Currently supports uploading one image and applying built-in effects to generate a processed version for comparison. To compare two different images, process them in another tool first, then use the side-by-side comparison feature.
imageTools.compareFaq2Q: How does the slider mode work?
imageTools.compareFaq2A: In slider mode, a divider line appears in the middle of the image. Drag the divider left or right: the left side shows the original, the right side shows the processed result. This allows area-by-area comparison.
imageTools.compareFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG for best quality.
imageTools.toPdfFeatureDesc: Our online image to PDF converter lets you convert multiple images into a PDF document, with each image on its own page. Supports reordering images. Ideal for digital albums, document archiving, and image organization. All operations are performed locally in your browser.
imageTools.toPdfFeature1Desc: Select multiple images at once and convert them all into a single PDF
imageTools.toPdfFeature2Desc: Adjust the order of images in the PDF by rearranging them
imageTools.toPdfFeature3Desc: Images are automatically centered and scaled to fit A4 page size
imageTools.toPdfStep1: Click the Select Images button and choose one or more images
imageTools.toPdfStep2: Use the arrow buttons on each image to adjust the order
imageTools.toPdfStep3: Click the Generate PDF button and wait for processing
imageTools.toPdfStep4: Preview the PDF and click Download PDF to save locally
imageTools.toPdfFaq1Q: What size is the generated PDF?
imageTools.toPdfFaq1A: The PDF page size is standard A4 (210x297mm). Images are automatically scaled and centered to fit the page. For custom sizes, use professional layout software.
imageTools.toPdfFaq2Q: How many images can I convert at once?
imageTools.toPdfFaq2A: There is no strict limit, but for browser performance we recommend no more than 20 images per session. Each image generates one PDF page.
imageTools.toPdfFaq3Q: Are images uploaded to a server?
imageTools.toPdfFaq3A: No. All image processing and PDF generation happens locally in your browser. Your image data never leaves your device, ensuring complete privacy.
imageTools.bgRemoveFeatureDesc: Our online background removal tool lets you remove image backgrounds by selecting the target color. Ideal for product displays, avatar creation, and material extraction. Adjust tolerance for precise control. All operations are performed locally in your browser.
imageTools.bgRemoveFeature1Desc: Pick the background color using a color picker or by clicking directly on the image
imageTools.bgRemoveFeature2Desc: Adjustable tolerance from 5-150 for precise color removal range control
imageTools.bgRemoveFeature3Desc: Outputs PNG format with transparent background for easy compositing
imageTools.bgRemoveStep1: Click the upload button to select an image for background removal
imageTools.bgRemoveStep2: Use the color picker or click the image to select the background color to remove
imageTools.bgRemoveStep3: Adjust the tolerance slider to control the color removal range
imageTools.bgRemoveStep4: Click Remove Background, preview the result, and download the PNG image
imageTools.bgRemoveFaq1Q: What type of images work best?
imageTools.bgRemoveFaq1A: This tool uses color-based removal, making it ideal for images with solid color backgrounds (e.g., white or green screen). Results may be less effective on complex backgrounds or when foreground colors are similar to the background.
imageTools.bgRemoveFaq2Q: How can I get better results?
imageTools.bgRemoveFaq2A: Tips: 1) Use a solid color background; 2) Ensure good contrast between foreground and background; 3) Adjust tolerance to find the optimal balance; 4) Try different color values if results are unsatisfactory.
imageTools.bgRemoveFaq3A: All common image formats are supported for input. Output format is PNG with alpha channel for transparent backgrounds.

textTools.whitespaceFeatureDesc: Our online whitespace tool helps you quickly clean up extra whitespace in text. Supports trimming leading/trailing whitespace, compressing multiple spaces, removing empty lines, and removing all whitespace. Real-time before/after character and line count comparison.

textTools.whitespaceFeature2Desc: See character and line count changes in real-time between original and processed text

textTools.whitespaceStep4: Review statistics and copy the processed result
textTools.whitespaceFaq1Q: Does removing all whitespace delete spaces between words?
textTools.whitespaceFaq1A: Yes. The Remove All option strips every whitespace character including word separators. For preserving word spacing, use the other options instead.
textTools.whitespaceFaq2A: Yes, the first four options can be freely combined. Remove All is a standalone mode and will disable other options when enabled.
textTools.whitespaceFaq3A: Whitespace processing is irreversible. Keep a copy of your original text before processing, or preserve the input until you are satisfied with the result.
textTools.dedupFeatureDesc: Our online text deduplication tool quickly removes duplicate lines from your text. Supports case-insensitive comparison, whitespace trimming, and result sorting. Shows real-time statistics for total, unique, and removed lines.

textTools.dedupFeature2Desc: Trim whitespace before comparing, ignore case, or sort results alphabetically
textTools.dedupFeature3Desc: Real-time display of total lines, unique lines, and removed lines count
textTools.dedupStep1: Paste or enter text with duplicate lines in the input box

textTools.dedupStep3: View real-time deduplicated result and statistics
textTools.dedupStep4: Copy the deduplicated result with one click
textTools.dedupFaq1Q: Is deduplication exact or fuzzy matching?
textTools.dedupFaq1A: Exact line matching by default. When Ignore Case is enabled, lines differing only in case are treated as duplicates. Trim Whitespace strips leading/trailing whitespace before comparing.
textTools.dedupFaq2Q: Which occurrence of a duplicate is kept?
textTools.dedupFaq2A: The first occurrence is kept. Subsequent duplicates are removed. Sorting changes the original line order.
textTools.dedupFaq3A: Original order is preserved by default. Enable Sort Results to reorder alphabetically. Disable sorting to keep the original sequence.

textTools.extractInfoFaq1A: Extraction uses regular expression patterns. Standard formats like example@mail.com or https://example.com are reliably recognized. Non-standard formats may not be detected.
textTools.extractInfoFaq2A: Yes. Duplicate results within the same type are automatically removed, ensuring each unique value appears only once.
textTools.extractInfoFaq3A: IPv6 extraction is not supported in the current version. For IPv4, invalid segments exceeding 255 are automatically filtered out.

textTools.markdownToHtmlFaq2A: This tool only converts Markdown to HTML. For reverse conversion, you would need a separate HTML to Markdown converter.

textTools.lineSortFeatureDesc: Our online line sort tool sorts text lines alphabetically, numerically, or by length. Supports deduplication, case-insensitive sorting, and trimming whitespace. Ideal for organizing lists, cleaning data, and preparing structured text.

textTools.lineSortFaq1A: Lines starting with numbers are sorted numerically. Non-numeric lines fall back to alphabetical sorting.
textTools.lineSortFaq2A: When "Ignore Case" is enabled, deduplication treats "Apple" and "apple" as duplicates. Otherwise, they are distinct.
textTools.lineSortFaq3A: Yes, but each CSV row should be on a separate line. For column-based sorting, use a spreadsheet tool.

textTools.traditionalSimplifiedFaq2Q: Can it convert mixed English and Chinese?
textTools.traditionalSimplifiedFaq2A: Yes. English characters and numbers remain unchanged. Only Chinese characters are converted based on the mapping table.
textTools.traditionalSimplifiedFaq3A: Accuracy depends on vocabulary coverage. Context-dependent conversions (e.g., "发" as "髮" vs "發") may need manual review.
textTools.escapeFeatureDesc: Our online escape/unescape tool supports HTML entity encoding/decoding and JavaScript string escaping/unescaping. Handles special characters, quotes, newlines, tabs, and control characters. Bidirectional conversion with one-click mode switching.
textTools.escapeFeature1Desc: HTML entities and JavaScript string escaping for common use cases

textTools.escapeStep4: Click "Convert" and copy result from right panel
textTools.escapeFaq1Q: Difference between HTML and JS escaping?
textTools.escapeFaq1A: HTML escaping converts to entities (e.g., < to <) for safe display. JS escaping uses backslash (e.g., " to \") for string literals.
textTools.escapeFaq2Q: Can unescape restore all escaped content?
textTools.escapeFaq2A: Standard entities and escape sequences restore perfectly. Non-standard or nested escapes may not fully restore.
textTools.escapeFaq3Q: What control characters are supported in JS mode?
textTools.escapeFaq3A: JS mode handles \n \r \t \b \f for newline, carriage return, tab, backspace, and form feed respectively.

textTools.lineNumberFaq1A: No. With "Skip Empty Lines", empty lines keep their position but get no number. Subsequent lines continue numbering.
textTools.lineNumberFaq2A: Minimum is 1. For zero-based numbering, generate then manually adjust.
textTools.lineNumberFaq3Q: Can I customize the number format?
textTools.lineNumberFaq3A: Currently 4 preset formats. For custom formats, copy result and modify manually.
textTools.trimTextFeatureDesc: Our online text truncation tool cuts text by character count or line count. Customizable suffix (default ...). Useful for excerpts, previews, and length-limited fields. All processing is local.

textTools.trimTextFaq2Q: Is suffix counted in length limit?
textTools.trimTextFaq2A: No. Limit applies to retained text only. Suffix is appended after truncation.
textTools.trimTextFaq3Q: How does line truncation handle partial lines?
textTools.trimTextFaq3A: Line mode keeps first N complete lines, then appends suffix. If text has fewer lines, no truncation occurs.

textTools.mergeLinesFaq3Q: Order of trim and remove empty?
textTools.mergeLinesFaq3A: First trim each line, then remove empty lines, finally join with delimiter.

devTools.colorPickerFaq1A: Yes. Click or drag on the palette to pick colors. The horizontal axis controls saturation, vertical axis controls lightness.
devTools.colorPickerFaq2Q: How long is the color history preserved?
devTools.colorPickerFaq2A: Color history is saved in localStorage. It persists across browser sessions and stores up to 10 most recent colors.
devTools.colorPickerFaq3A: The current version mainly supports opaque colors. For transparent colors, use the Color Converter tool with RGBA format support.
devTools.gradientFeatureDesc: Our online CSS gradient generator helps you create beautiful linear and radial gradients with multiple color stops. Supports real-time preview, angle control, and one-click CSS code copy. All processing happens locally in your browser.

devTools.gradientFeature2Desc: Add up to 8 color stops, freely adjust colors and positions

devTools.gradientStep4: Copy the generated CSS code and use it in your project
devTools.gradientFaq1Q: Can I use the generated gradient anywhere?
devTools.gradientFaq1A: Yes. The generated CSS code is standard CSS gradient syntax, compatible with all modern browsers. Just copy and paste into your stylesheet.
devTools.gradientFaq2Q: What is the maximum number of color stops?
devTools.gradientFaq2A: You can add up to 8 color stops. Each stop has its own color and position control for creating complex gradients.
devTools.gradientFaq3Q: Are linear and radial gradients equally supported?
devTools.gradientFaq3A: Yes. Both gradient types support full color stop editing and custom positioning. Linear gradients add angle control, while radial gradients offer circle/ellipse shape options.
devTools.shadowFeatureDesc: Online CSS shadow generator that creates multi-layer shadow effects. Freely adjust X/Y offset, blur radius, spread, color and opacity with support for both inset and outset shadows. All processing happens locally in your browser. Generated CSS code is ready to use in your projects.
devTools.shadowFeature1Desc: Supports up to 5 shadow layers, each with independent control over all parameters for advanced visual effects
devTools.shadowFeature2Desc: See shadow effects instantly on a sample card, with light/dark/transparent background options
devTools.shadowFeature3Desc: Built-in presets including Soft, Medium, Hard, Neon Glow, and Inset for one-click application
devTools.shadowStep1: Choose a preset shadow or adjust parameters manually
devTools.shadowStep2: Set X/Y offset, blur radius, and spread range
devTools.shadowStep4: Copy the generated CSS code and use it in your project
devTools.shadowFaq1Q: Which browsers support the generated shadows?
devTools.shadowFaq1A: box-shadow is supported by all modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and IE9+. It is production-ready.
devTools.shadowFaq2Q: Can inset and outset shadows be used together?
devTools.shadowFaq2A: Yes. Each shadow layer can be independently set as inset or outset. Add multiple layers and configure each one for combined effects.
devTools.shadowFaq3Q: What does a negative spread do?
devTools.shadowFaq3A: A negative spread value shrinks the shadow inward, creating a tighter effect. Combined with positive blur, it creates edge glow or inner carve effects.
devTools.flexboxFeatureDesc: Online Flexbox layout visualizer that lets you configure all Flexbox container and item properties through a graphical interface. Supports flex-direction, flex-wrap, justify-content, align-items, gap, and more with real-time preview. All processing happens locally in your browser.

devTools.flexboxFeature2Desc: See items re-arrange instantly as you adjust properties for intuitive learning
devTools.flexboxFeature3Desc: Auto-generates compatible CSS code with one-click copy

devTools.flexboxStep2: Adjust justify-content and align-items for alignment control
devTools.flexboxStep3: Set gap and flex-wrap for spacing and wrapping
devTools.flexboxStep4: Fine-tune individual item properties: flex-grow, flex-shrink, align-self
devTools.flexboxFaq1Q: What is the difference between flex-grow and flex-shrink?
devTools.flexboxFaq1A: flex-grow controls how items grow when there is extra space on the main axis. flex-shrink controls how items shrink when space is limited. flex-basis sets the initial size. Together they determine the final item size.
devTools.flexboxFaq2Q: What is the difference between align-items and align-content?
devTools.flexboxFaq2A: align-items controls alignment of items within a single row/column. align-content only works with flex-wrap: wrap when there are multiple rows/columns, controlling how rows are distributed on the cross axis.
devTools.flexboxFaq3Q: What is the browser support for the gap property?
devTools.flexboxFaq3A: gap is now supported in all modern browsers for Flexbox (Chrome 84+, Firefox 63+, Safari 14.1+, Edge 84+). For older browsers, use margin-based alternatives.
devTools.gridLayoutFeatureDesc: Online CSS Grid layout visualizer that lets you configure grid containers and items through a graphical interface. Supports custom column/row templates, gaps, alignment, and item spanning. Includes preset layout templates. All processing happens locally in your browser.
devTools.gridLayoutFeature1Desc: Pre-built layouts: 3-column equal, sidebar, holy grail, dashboard - get started instantly
devTools.gridLayoutFeature2Desc: Custom columns, rows, gaps, alignment, and per-item column/row spanning
devTools.gridLayoutFeature3Desc: Toggle grid line visibility to visualize the grid structure and item placement
devTools.gridLayoutStep1: Choose a preset grid template or customize column/row settings
devTools.gridLayoutStep2: Adjust gap to control spacing between grid cells
devTools.gridLayoutStep3: Set justify-items and align-items for item alignment within cells
devTools.gridLayoutStep4: Adjust col-span and row-span for each item to see spanning effects
devTools.gridLayoutFaq1A: 1fr represents one fraction of the available space. grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr creates three equal-width columns. It can be mixed with fixed widths, e.g., 200px 1fr means first column is 200px, rest goes to second.
devTools.gridLayoutFaq2Q: Grid vs Flexbox: which to choose?
devTools.gridLayoutFaq2A: Grid is best for 2D layouts (rows and columns simultaneously). Flexbox is best for 1D layouts (single row or column). Grid excels at page architecture, Flexbox at component-level arrangement.
devTools.gridLayoutFaq3Q: How to make items span multiple columns?
devTools.gridLayoutFaq3A: Use grid-column: span N and grid-row: span N. Adjust the col-span and row-span controls for each item to create spanning effects across the grid.
devTools.gridGeneratorFeatureDesc: Online grid background generator that creates square, dot, and isometric grid patterns. Customize grid size, line color, background color, line width, and opacity. Generated CSS can be used as web page backgrounds. Also supports PNG export. All processing happens locally in your browser.

devTools.gridGeneratorFaq1Q: Can the generated grid be used as a web background?
devTools.gridGeneratorFaq1A: Yes. The generated CSS background property works on any HTML element and is compatible with all modern browsers.
devTools.gridGeneratorFaq2Q: What angle is the isometric grid?
devTools.gridGeneratorFaq2A: The isometric grid uses 30-degree and 150-degree intersecting lines for a standard isometric projection, commonly used in game development and architectural design.
devTools.gridGeneratorFaq3Q: What resolution is the exported PNG?
devTools.gridGeneratorFaq3A: The exported PNG is 800x600 pixels. For different sizes, use an image resizer tool after export.

devTools.cssVariableFeature2Desc: Paste CSS variable definitions for bulk import, export all variables as a :root block with one click

devTools.cssVariableStep4: Use import/export for batch variable management
devTools.cssVariableFaq1A: Variables are stored in browser localStorage. They persist across sessions but may be lost if the browser cache is cleared. Regular export backup is recommended.
devTools.cssVariableFaq2Q: Do I need to add the -- prefix to variable names?
devTools.cssVariableFaq2A: No. The tool automatically prepends -- to variable names. Just enter the name body (e.g., primary-color becomes --primary-color).
devTools.cssVariableFaq3Q: Does it support @media or theme switching?
devTools.cssVariableFaq3A: The current version manages :root-level global variables. For dark theme or scoped variables, use multiple variable sets or manage them separately.

devTools.responsiveTestStep4: Switch view counts to compare layout across different sizes
devTools.responsiveTestFaq1Q: Why do some sites fail to load?
devTools.responsiveTestFaq1A: Some sites block iframe loading via X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy headers. Use the custom HTML mode for testing your own content.
devTools.responsiveTestFaq2A: Touch events in iframe-loaded pages are handled natively by the browser. Custom HTML mode does not simulate touch events - test on real devices for touch interactions.
devTools.responsiveTestFaq3Q: Can I test local development servers?
devTools.responsiveTestFaq3A: If the local server is on the same origin or has proper CORS headers configured, it can be loaded. Use localhost or 127.0.0.1 for access.
devTools.contrastCheckFeatureDesc: Online WCAG contrast checker that calculates the contrast ratio between foreground and background colors per WCAG 2.1 standards. Displays AA/AAA compliance levels and suggests color adjustments to meet accessibility requirements. All calculations happen locally in your browser.
devTools.contrastCheckFeature1Desc: Instant contrast ratio and WCAG compliance display as you pick colors
devTools.contrastCheckFeature2Desc: Auto-generates lighter/darker alternatives to help meet accessibility standards quickly
devTools.contrastCheckFeature3Desc: Grayscale simulation to check readability for color-blind users
devTools.contrastCheckStep2: View real-time contrast ratio and WCAG compliance levels
devTools.contrastCheckStep3: If failing, use color suggestions to adjust colors
devTools.contrastCheckStep4: Use the swap button to quickly exchange foreground and background
devTools.contrastCheckFaq1A: WCAG AA requires at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. This is the internationally accepted accessibility standard. All public-facing websites are encouraged to comply.
devTools.contrastCheckFaq2Q: Are large and small text standards the same?
devTools.contrastCheckFaq2A: No. Large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold) has lower requirements: AA is 3:1, AAA is 4.5:1. Normal text requires higher: AA is 4.5:1, AAA is 7:1.
devTools.contrastCheckFaq3Q: Are suggested colors guaranteed to work?
devTools.contrastCheckFaq3A: Suggested colors are based solely on luminance adjustment to meet contrast ratios. Consider your brand palette and design style when choosing the final color.
devTools.radixFeatureDesc: Online radix converter supporting binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal formats. Enter a value in any base and see real-time conversions to all others. Ideal for programming, network engineering, and digital electronics. All conversion happens locally in your browser.
devTools.radixFeature1Desc: Binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal real-time conversion covering common use cases
devTools.radixFeature2Desc: Edit any base input and all others update instantly
devTools.radixFeature3Desc: Automatic validation of input characters with error hints for invalid entries

devTools.radixStep3: Click the copy button to copy the result in any base
devTools.radixStep4: Paste values directly into any input field for instant conversion
devTools.radixFaq1Q: Why does binary only use 0 and 1?
devTools.radixFaq1A: Binary is the most fundamental number system in computers, using only 0 and 1 to represent electrical on/off states. All computer data is ultimately stored and processed in binary.
devTools.radixFaq2A: Hexadecimal uses 0-9 and A-F to represent values 0-15, since a single decimal digit can't represent 10-15. A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15. Case insensitive.
devTools.radixFaq3A: 0b indicates binary (e.g., 0b1010), 0o indicates octal (e.g., 0o12), and 0x indicates hexadecimal (e.g., 0xFF). These are common notations in programming languages. Decimal numbers typically have no prefix.
devTools.timestampFeatureDesc: Online Unix timestamp converter for bidirectional conversion between timestamps and human-readable dates. Displays ISO format, local format, UTC format, and relative time. Supports both seconds and milliseconds. All processing is local in your browser.
devTools.timestampFeature1Desc: Convert timestamps to dates and dates to timestamps in real time
devTools.timestampFeature2Desc: View ISO, local, UTC, and relative time formats simultaneously
devTools.timestampFeature3Desc: Toggle between seconds (10-digit) and milliseconds (13-digit) timestamps
devTools.timestampStep1: Enter a Unix timestamp or pick a date/time
devTools.timestampStep2: Toggle seconds/milliseconds and view corresponding date formats
devTools.timestampStep3: Click Now to insert the current timestamp instantly
devTools.timestampStep4: Click copy on any format to copy the result
devTools.timestampFaq1A: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds (10 digits) or milliseconds (13 digits) since January 1, 1970 (UTC). It is a timezone-independent time representation widely used in computer systems.
devTools.timestampFaq2Q: What is the difference between seconds and milliseconds?
devTools.timestampFaq2A: Second timestamps are 10 digits (e.g., 1704067200), millisecond timestamps are 13 digits (e.g., 1704067200000). JavaScript's Date.now() returns milliseconds. Millisecond precision is 1000x finer.
devTools.timestampFaq3A: 32-bit systems store timestamps as signed integers. The maximum value corresponds to January 19, 2038. After that, overflow occurs. 64-bit systems have solved this. This tool supports 64-bit timestamps.
devTools.unitConvertFeatureDesc: Online unit converter supporting 8 categories: length, weight, temperature, area, volume, speed, time, and data storage. Standard conversion factors with precise results. All calculations happen locally in your browser.
devTools.unitConvertFeature1Desc: Length, weight, temperature, area, volume, speed, time, and data - covers daily and office scenarios
devTools.unitConvertFeature2Desc: Standard conversion factors with special formulas for temperature and other non-linear units
devTools.unitConvertFeature3Desc: Common conversion examples displayed below each category for quick reference
devTools.unitConvertStep1: Select a conversion category (Length, Weight, etc.)
devTools.unitConvertStep3: Enter a value in either input field for real-time conversion
devTools.unitConvertStep4: Click swap to quickly exchange source and target units
devTools.unitConvertFaq1A: Temperature uses formulas: Celsius to Fahrenheit F=Cx9/5+32, Fahrenheit to Celsius C=(F-32)x5/9, Kelvin K=C+273.15. Unlike other units that use simple multiplication.
devTools.unitConvertFaq2Q: What standard does data storage use?
devTools.unitConvertFaq2A: This tool uses binary (1024-base): 1KB=1024B, 1MB=1024KB, etc. Hard drive manufacturers often use 1000-base, so a 1TB drive shows as ~931GB.
devTools.unitConvertFaq3Q: How many decimal places are shown?
devTools.unitConvertFaq3A: Results auto-select appropriate decimal places (up to 10). Very large or small values use scientific notation.
devTools.dateCalcFeatureDesc: Online date calculator for calculating date differences and performing date arithmetic. Compute differences in days, weeks, months, years, hours, and minutes. Add or subtract time from any date. All processing happens locally in your browser.

devTools.dateCalcFeature2Desc: Add or subtract years, months, days from any start date
devTools.dateCalcFeature3Desc: View results in days, weeks, months, years, hours, and minutes simultaneously

devTools.dateCalcStep3: In Add/Subtract mode, enter base date and adjustment values
devTools.dateCalcStep4: View results and click copy to get date values
devTools.dateCalcFaq1Q: Does the day difference include the end date?
devTools.dateCalcFaq1A: No. The difference counts complete days between start and end dates, excluding the end date. For example, January 1 to January 2 is a difference of 1 day.
devTools.dateCalcFaq2Q: Are there precision issues with month arithmetic?
devTools.dateCalcFaq2A: Month arithmetic uses JavaScript's setMonth method. If the target month has fewer days than the current day (e.g., Jan 31 plus 1 month), it auto-adjusts to the last day of the target month.
devTools.dateCalcFaq3A: Yes. JavaScript's Date object automatically handles leap years. February 29 displays correctly in leap years, and day difference calculations properly account for the extra day.
devTools.mimeQueryFeatureDesc: Online MIME type lookup tool supporting bidirectional search by file extension or MIME type. Includes a database of 130+ common MIME types covering documents, images, audio, video, fonts, archives, and more. All queries happen locally in your browser.

devTools.mimeQueryFeature2Desc: Automatically detects whether your input is an extension or MIME type for smart matching
devTools.mimeQueryFeature3Desc: Filter by categories like application, image, text, video, audio for quick navigation

devTools.mimeQueryFaq1A: MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) types are a standard for identifying file formats and data types. Format: type/subtype, e.g., text/html, image/jpeg. Browsers and servers use MIME types to handle files correctly.
devTools.mimeQueryFaq2Q: Does the database include all MIME types?
devTools.mimeQueryFaq2A: This tool includes 130+ most common MIME types covering daily development needs. IANA registers thousands of types. For special needs, refer to the official IANA registry.
devTools.mimeQueryFaq3Q: Do extensions in results include the dot?
devTools.mimeQueryFaq3A: Extensions display with the dot prefix (e.g., .pdf) for easy copying. Input works with or without the dot.

devTools.jsonFormatFaq2A: Recommended under 10MB. Larger files may cause browser lag. For huge JSON files, use streaming parsers.
devTools.jsonFormatFaq3Q: Can I format JSON with comments?
devTools.jsonFormatFaq3A: Standard JSON doesn't support comments. The tool will show an error. Remove comments before formatting.

devTools.colorConvertFaq1Q: What is the difference between HSL and HSV?
devTools.colorConvertFaq1A: HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) is symmetric for light/dark. HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value) is more intuitive for color picking. Both represent the same color space differently.
devTools.colorConvertFaq2Q: Is CMYK conversion accurate for print?
devTools.colorConvertFaq2A: RGB to CMYK conversion is approximate. Print CMYK depends on color profile (SWOP, FOGRA, etc.). For professional print, use ICC profiles in design software.
devTools.colorConvertFaq3Q: How to copy a color value?
devTools.colorConvertFaq3A: Each format row has a copy button. Click to copy the formatted value to clipboard.

devTools.yamlToJsonFaq1A: Yes. Anchors (&) and aliases (*) are resolved during YAML to JSON conversion. JSON to YAML creates anchors for repeated objects.
devTools.yamlToJsonFaq2A: Yes. Documents separated by --- are converted to a JSON array. For single document, use single file.

devTools.csvToJsonFaq1Q: How to handle fields containing delimiters?
devTools.csvToJsonFaq1A: Fields containing delimiter, quote, or newline are auto-wrapped in double quotes and internal quotes escaped (RFC 4180). Ensure input CSV is valid.
devTools.csvToJsonFaq2A: Recommended under 100k rows. Larger files may cause browser lag. Use streaming parsers for big data.
devTools.csvToJsonFaq3Q: Can converted JSON be converted back to original CSV?
devTools.csvToJsonFaq3A: Use JSON to CSV with same delimiter. Perfect restore only if original had no delimiter in field content. Use standard CSV for best results.

devTools.urlEncodeFeatureDesc: Online URL encode/decode tool using encodeURIComponent and decodeURIComponent. Safely encodes special characters for URL transmission, supporting Unicode characters including Chinese, Japanese, etc. All processing happens locally in your browser.
devTools.urlEncodeFeature1Desc: One-click switch between URL encoding and decoding modes
devTools.urlEncodeFeature2Desc: Perfectly handles Chinese, Japanese, emoji and other Unicode characters

devTools.urlEncodeStep4: Result appears on the right, click to copy
devTools.urlEncodeFaq1Q: What characters does URL encoding convert?
devTools.urlEncodeFaq1A: All characters except A-Z, a-z, 0-9, -, _, ., ~ are percent-encoded. Spaces become %20 (not +).
devTools.urlEncodeFaq2Q: When to use encodeURI vs encodeURIComponent?
devTools.urlEncodeFaq2A: encodeURI preserves URL structure chars (/, :, @, etc). encodeURIComponent encodes everything for query parameter values. This tool uses encodeURIComponent.
devTools.urlEncodeFaq3Q: Can I decode a full URL?
devTools.urlEncodeFaq3A: Yes, but it will decode the entire string. For query parameters only, extract the parameter value first, then decode.
devTools.base64TextFeatureDesc: Online Base64 encode/decode tool for bidirectional text and Base64 conversion. Base64 encodes binary data into printable ASCII, commonly used for email attachments, data URIs, and API data transfer. All processing is local in your browser.

devTools.base64TextFeature2Desc: Handles mixed Chinese/English text perfectly with UTF-8 encoding
devTools.base64TextFeature3Desc: Supports long text encoding/decoding (limited by browser memory)

devTools.base64TextStep4: Result appears on the right, click to copy
devTools.base64TextFaq1A: Yes. Base64 increases data size by ~33% since it uses 64 printable chars to represent 256 byte values.
devTools.base64TextFaq2A: Ensure input is standard Base64 (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /, =). Remove any whitespace or newlines before retrying.
devTools.base64TextFaq3A: This tool only handles text. For image to Base64, use the Image to Base64 tool in Image Processing category.
devTools.md5FeatureDesc: Online MD5 hash calculator generates 128-bit (32 hex chars) message digest. MD5 is commonly used for file integrity verification and data fingerprinting. All computation happens locally in your browser - input never leaves your device.
devTools.md5Feature1Desc: Web Crypto API generates MD5 hash in milliseconds
devTools.md5Feature2Desc: Pure local computation, your input text never leaves your device
devTools.md5Feature3Desc: Outputs standard 32-char lowercase hex hash compatible with all systems

devTools.md5Step4: Click Clear to compute hash for new text
devTools.md5Faq1Q: Can MD5 be reversed to get original text?
devTools.md5Faq1A: No. MD5 is a one-way hash function - irreversible. Same input always produces same output, but output cannot derive input.
devTools.md5Faq2Q: Is MD5 suitable for password storage?
devTools.md5Faq2A: No. MD5 is too fast and vulnerable to rainbow tables. Use bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 for password hashing.
devTools.md5Faq3A: Compute MD5 of downloaded file and compare with official checksum. Match means file is intact and unmodified.
devTools.shaFeatureDesc: Online SHA hash calculator supporting SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512. SHA family is more secure than MD5. SHA-256 is widely used in blockchain, SSL certificates, and password storage. All computation is local in your browser.
devTools.shaFeature1Desc: Switch between SHA-1/256/384/512 for different security levels
devTools.shaFeature2Desc: SHA-256+ offers strong collision resistance for security-critical applications
devTools.shaFeature3Desc: Web Crypto API based, data never leaves browser, absolute privacy
devTools.shaStep1: Paste or enter text to hash in the input box
devTools.shaStep2: Select algorithm: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, or SHA-512
devTools.shaStep4: Result appears on the right, click to copy
devTools.shaFaq1A: SHA-1 has known collision attacks. Not recommended for digital signatures or SSL. Use SHA-256 or higher instead.
devTools.shaFaq2Q: SHA-256 vs SHA-512: which is more secure?
devTools.shaFaq2A: SHA-512 has longer output (512 vs 256 bits) and theoretically stronger collision resistance. On 64-bit systems SHA-512 is faster. SHA-256 is generally recommended for balance.
devTools.shaFaq3A: This tool only handles text input. For file hashing, use the File Hash tool in File Processing category with drag-and-drop support.
devTools.uuidFeatureDesc: Online UUID/GUID generator supporting standard UUID v4, Windows GUID (uppercase with braces), and short UUID formats. UUID v4 uses cryptographically secure random with 122 bits of entropy, making collisions practically impossible. Ideal for database keys, distributed IDs, session tokens.
devTools.uuidFeature1Desc: Standard UUID, Windows GUID, Short UUID - one-click switch covers all common use cases
devTools.uuidFeature2Desc: Generate up to 100 UUIDs at once for development and testing efficiency
devTools.uuidFeature3Desc: Uses crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.getRandomValues() for guaranteed uniqueness
devTools.uuidStep1: Select UUID format: Standard, GUID, or Short
devTools.uuidStep4: Results listed on the right, click copy all
devTools.uuidFaq1A: Theoretically possible but practically impossible. v4 UUID has 2^122 combinations. Generating 1 billion/sec for 100 years gives ~50% collision chance.
devTools.uuidFaq2A: GUID is Microsoft's UUID implementation - uppercase with braces 〔XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX〕. Same standard, different format.
devTools.uuidFaq3A: Short UUID removes fixed version/variant bits, keeping ~118 bits entropy. Still sufficient for virtually all use cases.

devTools.passwordFaq1A: No. All generation happens in-browser. Passwords never sent to server, not stored in localStorage or cookies.
devTools.passwordFaq2A: Use a password manager (Bitwarden, 1Password, KeePass). Never screenshot, keep in clipboard long-term, or share.

devTools.regexTestFaq1A: No. Enter the pattern only (for example: backslash d plus or caret [a-z] plus dollar). Flags are selected via checkboxes. The tool constructs the RegExp internally.
devTools.regexTestFaq2A: Enable the s (dotAll) flag so dot matches newlines. Or use character classes like backslash s backslash S or backslash d backslash D.
devTools.regexTestFaq3A: Common issues: 1) Unescaped special characters (dot, asterisk, plus, question, caret, dollar, parentheses, brackets, braces, pipe, backslash). 2) Wrong flags (example: missing g for global). 3) Syntax error (red error indicator shows details).

Generates railroad diagram for structure flow, supports live match testing, and includes common presets. Helps understand complex regex logic, debug patterns, and learn regex syntax. devTools.regexVisualFaq2Q: How to read the railroad diagram?
devTools.regexVisualFaq2A: Follow paths left-to-right. Solid boxes=literals, dashed boxes=char classes, diamonds=branch choices, loop arrows=quantifiers. Colors match token types: purple=anchors, orange=quantifiers, green=groups, red=alternation, cyan=char classes, pink=escapes.
devTools.regexVisualFaq3A: 1) Check red error tokens 2) Verify branch/loop structure in diagram 3) Simplify stepwise, test each step in match area 4) Compare expected vs actual token types.

efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq1Q: Are the generated numbers truly random?
efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq1A: This tool uses JavaScript's Math.random() which generates pseudo-random numbers suitable for most everyday scenarios. For cryptographically secure random numbers, use specialized tools.
efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq2Q: What if the range is too small when duplicates are disabled?
efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq2A: When duplicates are disabled, the count cannot exceed the range size. The button will be automatically disabled if the range is insufficient.
efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq3Q: Does it support negative numbers and decimals?
efficiencyTools.randomNumFaq3A: Negative numbers are supported. Decimals are not supported - input values are rounded to integers. For decimal random numbers, generate integers and divide manually.
efficiencyTools.radixCalcFeatureDesc: Our online radix calculator supports conversion and computation between any bases from 2 to 36. Enter a value in any base to see real-time conversion results for common bases (binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal), or expand to view all 35 bases.

efficiencyTools.radixCalcFaq1Q: What is the maximum supported value?
efficiencyTools.radixCalcFaq1A: Based on JavaScript Number type, supports integers up to approximately 2^53. Values beyond this range may lose precision.
efficiencyTools.radixCalcFaq2A: Base 36 uses digits 0-9 and letters A-Z, totaling 36 characters. This is the maximum base possible with case-insensitive alphanumeric characters. Bases beyond 36 typically use Unicode symbols.
efficiencyTools.radixCalcFaq3Q: What happens if I enter invalid characters?
efficiencyTools.radixCalcFaq3A: If a character is outside the valid range for the selected base, the tool displays a red error hint. For example, entering 2 in binary or A in decimal.

efficiencyTools.qrcodeFaq2A: A QR code can hold up to 4,296 alphanumeric characters or 7,089 digits. Higher error correction reduces capacity.

efficiencyTools.barcodeFaq1Q: Which barcode format is most universal?
efficiencyTools.barcodeFaq1A: CODE128 is the most versatile, supporting the full ASCII character set with high encoding density. Widely used in logistics and inventory.
efficiencyTools.barcodeFaq2A: EAN-13 is the international standard (13 digits), UPC-A is US/Canada standard (12 digits). EAN-13 is compatible with UPC-A.
efficiencyTools.barcodeFaq3Q: Is there a length limit for barcode content?
efficiencyTools.barcodeFaq3A: Limits vary by format. CODE128 supports up to 80 characters, EAN-13 is fixed at 13 digits. Choose the format that fits your use case.

efficiencyTools.passwordStrengthFaq2A: A strong password typically has: at least 12 characters, mixed case, numbers and special symbols, no consecutive repeated characters, no common words or personal info.
efficiencyTools.passwordStrengthFaq3Q: Is the crack time estimate accurate?
efficiencyTools.passwordStrengthFaq3A: Crack time is estimated at 1 billion guesses per second. Actual time depends on attacker's hardware and methods. Use as reference only and change passwords regularly.

efficiencyTools.notepadFaq2Q: Will my content persist after closing the page?
efficiencyTools.notepadFaq2A: Yes. Content auto-saves and persists across page refreshes and browser restarts within the same browser. Different browsers do not share data.

efficiencyTools.countdownStep1: Enter an event name, select target date and time
efficiencyTools.countdownStep3: View remaining days, hours, minutes, and seconds in real-time
efficiencyTools.countdownStep4: Alarm plays when time's up, click "Reset" to start over
efficiencyTools.countdownFaq1A: The countdown is accurate to the second, updating every second. For higher precision, use the Stopwatch tool.
efficiencyTools.countdownFaq2Q: Can I run multiple countdowns simultaneously?
efficiencyTools.countdownFaq2A: One countdown at a time. Saved events can be quickly swapped and restarted.
efficiencyTools.countdownFaq3Q: Does the countdown persist after page refresh?
efficiencyTools.countdownFaq3A: Running countdowns stop on page refresh, but saved events remain in local storage for quick restart.
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFeatureDesc: Our online stopwatch is accurate to 10 milliseconds with start/pause/reset and lap recording. Ideal for sports timing, measurements, and productivity tracking.
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFeature1Desc: Accurate to 10 milliseconds for professional timing needs
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFeature2Desc: Record lap times with automatic best/worst lap highlighting
efficiencyTools.stopwatchStep1: Click the play button to start timing
efficiencyTools.stopwatchStep2: Click "Lap" during timing to record split times
efficiencyTools.stopwatchStep3: Click pause to stop, play again to resume
efficiencyTools.stopwatchStep4: Click reset to clear, view all lap records
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFaq1A: Browser JavaScript timers have a minimum precision of about 4-10ms. 10ms display accuracy is the reliably achievable precision.
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFaq2Q: How many laps can I record?
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFaq2A: Unlimited laps. All records are displayed in reverse chronological order with best and worst laps highlighted.
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFaq3Q: Does the timer continue in background tabs?
efficiencyTools.stopwatchFaq3A: Yes, timing continues in background tabs, though browsers may reduce timer precision for background tabs to conserve resources.
efficiencyTools.worldClockFeatureDesc: Our world clock displays current time and date for 15 major global cities simultaneously. Add custom timezone cities as needed. Auto-refreshes every second for cross-timezone collaboration.
efficiencyTools.worldClockFeature1Desc: 15 preset global cities with simultaneous time and date display
efficiencyTools.worldClockFeature2Desc: Search and add any timezone city for personalized needs
efficiencyTools.worldClockFeature3Desc: Time auto-refreshes every second, no manual refresh needed
efficiencyTools.worldClockStep1: View current time for 15 preset global cities
efficiencyTools.worldClockStep2: Click "Add City" to search and add more timezone cities
efficiencyTools.worldClockStep3: Remove added cities with the X button
efficiencyTools.worldClockStep4: Time auto-refreshes every second for accurate reading
efficiencyTools.worldClockFaq1Q: Which cities are available by default?
efficiencyTools.worldClockFaq1A: 15 default cities: Beijing, Tokyo, Seoul, Singapore, Dubai, London, Paris, Berlin, Moscow, New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, Toronto, Sydney, Auckland.
efficiencyTools.worldClockFaq2A: Click "Add City" in the top-right, search for a city or timezone name, and select to add.
efficiencyTools.worldClockFaq3A: Custom cities are saved to browser local storage and persist after closing the page.
efficiencyTools.timezoneFeatureDesc: Our timezone converter helps you convert date and time between different timezones. Supports all IANA timezones, auto-calculates time differences, and features one-click source/target swap.
efficiencyTools.timezoneFeature1Desc: Supports all IANA timezone database entries worldwide
efficiencyTools.timezoneFeature2Desc: Results update instantly when selecting timezones or changing time, with automatic offset calculation
efficiencyTools.timezoneFeature3Desc: Swap source and target timezones instantly with one click
efficiencyTools.timezoneStep1: Select the date and time to convert
efficiencyTools.timezoneStep3: View the converted time and date in the target timezone
efficiencyTools.timezoneStep4: Click "Now" for current time, click swap button to exchange timezones
efficiencyTools.timezoneFaq1A: All IANA timezones are supported, covering all countries and regions worldwide with their complete list of major cities.
efficiencyTools.timezoneFaq2A: Yes. Results automatically adjust for Daylight Saving Time based on the selected date, ensuring accuracy.
efficiencyTools.timezoneFaq3Q: What does the swap button do?
efficiencyTools.timezoneFaq3A: The swap button exchanges source and target timezones, making it easy to view bidirectional conversion results without reselecting.

fileTools.zipFeatureDesc: Online file compression tool helps you package multiple files into ZIP format with selectable compression levels. All operations are completed locally in the browser without uploading files to any server.
fileTools.zipFeature1Desc: Select multiple files and package them into a ZIP archive at once
fileTools.zipFeature2Desc: Supports levels 0-9, from no compression (store only) to maximum compression
fileTools.zipFeature3Desc: All file processing is done locally in the browser; data never leaves your device
fileTools.zipStep1: Click the "Select Files" button to choose one or multiple files
fileTools.zipStep2: Review selected files in the list and remove unwanted ones
fileTools.zipStep3: Adjust the compression level slider (0=store only, 9=maximum compression)
fileTools.zipStep4: Click "Start Compress" and download the ZIP file when ready
fileTools.zipFaq1Q: What is the difference between compression levels?
fileTools.zipFaq1A: Level 0 is store only (no compression) — fast but large files. Levels 1-9 use the DEFLATE algorithm; higher levels give better compression but take longer. Level 6 is recommended for everyday use, balancing speed and compression ratio.
fileTools.zipFaq2A: All file types are supported including documents, images, videos, and audio. No file format restrictions.
fileTools.zipFaq3Q: Are files uploaded to a server?
fileTools.zipFaq3A: No. All compression is done locally in the browser. File data never leaves your device, ensuring complete privacy.
fileTools.unzipFeatureDesc: Online file extraction tool helps you extract ZIP files, view internal file lists, and download files individually or all at once. Supports standard ZIP format. All operations are performed locally in the browser.
fileTools.unzipFeature1Desc: Upload a ZIP file and it automatically parses and lists internal files
fileTools.unzipFeature2Desc: Download individual files or all files at once with one click
fileTools.unzipFeature3Desc: Files are extracted locally in the browser; no data is uploaded to servers
fileTools.unzipStep1: Click "Select ZIP File" to upload the file you want to extract
fileTools.unzipStep2: The system automatically parses the ZIP file and lists all internal files
fileTools.unzipStep3: Browse the file list and click download buttons for individual files
fileTools.unzipStep4: Or click "Download All" to download all files at once
fileTools.unzipFaq1A: Currently supports standard ZIP format extraction. RAR, 7z, and other formats are not yet supported.
fileTools.unzipFaq2Q: Will files be corrupted after extraction?
fileTools.unzipFaq2A: No. The extraction process does not modify the original ZIP file, and extracted files are identical to the originals. If extraction fails, the ZIP file may be corrupted.
fileTools.unzipFaq3A: Yes. JSZip supports UTF-8 encoded filenames, including Chinese, Japanese, and other multilingual filenames.
fileTools.previewFeatureDesc: Online file preview tool helps you quickly preview image and text file contents. Supports common image formats (JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, etc.) and text formats (TXT, JSON, XML, code files, etc.). All operations are performed locally in the browser.
fileTools.previewFeature1Desc: High-definition preview of common image formats including JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, etc.
fileTools.previewFeature2Desc: Preview plain text files including TXT, JSON, XML, code files, and more
fileTools.previewFeature3Desc: Displays basic information such as filename and file size
fileTools.previewStep1: Click "Select File" to choose the file you want to preview
fileTools.previewStep2: The system automatically detects the file type and displays preview content
fileTools.previewStep3: Images are shown in high-definition; text files are displayed in monospace font
fileTools.previewFaq1A: All browser-renderable image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP, and SVG.
fileTools.previewFaq2A: Common text and code formats including TXT, JSON, XML, HTML, CSS, JS, TS, Python, Java, CSV, YAML, Markdown, and more.
fileTools.previewFaq3Q: Are files uploaded to a server?
fileTools.previewFaq3A: No. All file preview is done locally in the browser. File data never leaves your device.
fileTools.fileHashFeatureDesc: Online file hash calculator helps you compute SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, and MD5 hash values for files. Suitable for file integrity verification, cryptographic applications, and more. All computation is done locally in the browser.
fileTools.fileHashFeature1Desc: Supports SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, and MD5 hash algorithms
fileTools.fileHashFeature2Desc: Select single or multiple algorithms to compute simultaneously; results displayed clearly
fileTools.fileHashFeature3Desc: Each result has a copy button for one-click copying of hash values
fileTools.fileHashStep1: Select the hash algorithms to compute (multiple selection available)
fileTools.fileHashStep2: Click "Select File" to upload the file for hash computation
fileTools.fileHashStep3: The system automatically computes hash values for selected algorithms
fileTools.fileHashStep4: View results and click the "Copy" button to copy hash values to clipboard
fileTools.fileHashFaq1A: MD5 has proven collision vulnerabilities and is not recommended for security-sensitive scenarios. Use SHA-256 or SHA-512 for security applications. MD5 is suitable for file integrity verification and other non-security use cases.
fileTools.fileHashFaq2A: File reading and hash computation happen on the browser's main thread. Large files (over 100MB) may cause brief page lag. Consider chunked processing for very large files.
fileTools.fileHashFaq3Q: Can all algorithms be computed simultaneously?
fileTools.fileHashFaq3A: Yes. When multiple algorithms are selected, the system computes each sequentially. The more algorithms selected, the longer the computation time. Select only what you need.
fileTools.editorFeatureDesc: Online code editor helps you write and edit code or text with multi-language syntax recognition. Features line numbers, Tab indentation, code download, and built-in language templates. All operations are performed locally in the browser.
fileTools.editorFeature1Desc: Automatically detects programming language from file extension; supports 20+ languages including JS, TS, Python, Java, etc.
fileTools.editorFeature2Desc: Automatic line number display for easy code navigation and reading
fileTools.editorFeature3Desc: Quick-start templates for JavaScript, Python, HTML, CSS, and other common languages
fileTools.editorStep1: Enter a filename in the input field (e.g., index.js) — language is auto-detected
fileTools.editorStep2: Write code in the editor area with Tab indentation support
fileTools.editorStep3: Or click a preset language template to get started quickly
fileTools.editorStep4: Click "Download" to save the file, or "Copy Code" to copy content to clipboard
fileTools.editorFaq1A: The current version provides basic language recognition and file extension mapping without full syntax highlighting. For professional code editing, we recommend VS Code or similar editors.
fileTools.editorFaq2A: Edited content is only stored in browser memory and will be lost on refresh or close. Please click "Download" in time to save locally.
fileTools.editorFaq3A: 20+ common languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, CSS, JSON, XML, Markdown, Python, Java, C++, C, C#, Go, Rust, PHP, Ruby, Swift, SQL, Shell, YAML, and more.

dataTools.csvEditorFeatureDesc: Online CSV editor helps you view, edit, and export CSV data. Supports file upload, manual paste, cell editing, and row/column management. All data is processed locally in the browser.
dataTools.csvEditorFeature1Desc: Upload CSV files or paste CSV text directly; auto-parses into a table
dataTools.csvEditorFeature2Desc: Edit cells, add/delete rows and columns with real-time updates
dataTools.csvEditorFeature3Desc: Download edited data as a CSV file with one click
dataTools.csvEditorStep1: Upload a CSV file or paste CSV text into the input area
dataTools.csvEditorStep2: Click "Parse CSV" to convert data into an editable table
dataTools.csvEditorStep3: Edit cell contents directly; add or delete rows/columns as needed
dataTools.csvEditorStep4: Click "Download CSV" to save your edited data locally
dataTools.csvEditorFaq1A: Standard comma-separated CSV with quote escaping is supported, as well as Tab-separated TSV format.
dataTools.csvEditorFaq2A: All edits are stored only in browser memory and will be lost on page refresh. Please download your data in time.
dataTools.csvEditorFaq3A: Yes. CSV files are read and written in UTF-8 encoding, properly supporting Chinese characters.
dataTools.jsonEditorFeatureDesc: Online JSON editor helps you edit, format, and browse JSON data. Supports syntax validation, tree view browsing, value editing, and one-click copy. All operations are performed locally in the browser.
dataTools.jsonEditorFeature1Desc: Format JSON for readability or compress to a single line with one click
dataTools.jsonEditorFeature2Desc: Browse JSON as a collapsible tree; click values to edit them inline
dataTools.jsonEditorFeature3Desc: Validate JSON syntax in real-time with precise error location
dataTools.jsonEditorStep1: Enter or paste JSON text in the editor area
dataTools.jsonEditorStep2: Click "Format" to beautify or "Validate" to check syntax
dataTools.jsonEditorStep3: Switch to "Tree View" to browse the data structure in collapsible form
dataTools.jsonEditorStep4: Click values in the tree to edit; copy or download when done
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq1Q: Does it support large JSON files?
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq1A: We recommend processing JSON files under 10MB. Larger files may cause browser lag. For large JSON, consider dedicated JSON tools.
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq2Q: Can I edit values in tree view?
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq2A: Yes. Click any value (string, number, boolean) in the tree view to enter edit mode. Press Enter to save or Esc to cancel.
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq3Q: Does it support JSON5 or comments?
dataTools.jsonEditorFaq3A: Currently standard JSON format is supported. JSON5 (with comments, unquoted keys, etc.) is not yet supported.
dataTools.chartFeatureDesc: Online chart generator helps you quickly create bar, line, and pie charts. Supports custom data labels and values with real-time preview. All data is processed locally in the browser.
dataTools.chartFeature1Desc: Supports bar, line, and pie charts — three common chart types
dataTools.chartFeature2Desc: Add, delete, and modify data items freely with instant chart updates
dataTools.chartFeature3Desc: See chart updates immediately when changing data or switching chart types
dataTools.chartStep1: Select chart type: bar, line, or pie
dataTools.chartStep2: Enter labels and values in the data table
dataTools.chartStep3: Click "Add Data" to include more data items
dataTools.chartStep4: The chart renders in real-time for immediate viewing
dataTools.chartFaq1A: The current version supports real-time viewing in the browser. To save, use screenshot tools to capture the chart area.
dataTools.chartFaq2A: We recommend no more than 50 data points. Excessive data points reduce chart readability.
dataTools.chartFaq3A: Bar and line charts support negative values. Pie charts do not support negatives (negative values are converted to absolute values).
dataTools.statisticsFeatureDesc: Online statistical analysis tool helps you quickly compute basic statistics for your data. Supports 12 statistical metrics including mean, median, mode, variance, and standard deviation. All computation is done locally in the browser.
dataTools.statisticsFeature1Desc: Supports newline, comma, space, and other separators for data input
dataTools.statisticsFeature2Desc: Provides 12 statistical indicators: count, sum, mean, median, mode, range, variance, std deviation, etc.
dataTools.statisticsFeature3Desc: Auto-computes all statistics as you type, no button clicking needed
dataTools.statisticsStep1: Enter numbers in the input area, one per line or comma/space separated
dataTools.statisticsStep2: The system automatically identifies and parses valid numbers
dataTools.statisticsStep3: All statistical indicators are computed and displayed in real time
dataTools.statisticsStep4: Review results; clear input to start over
dataTools.statisticsFaq1Q: How much data can I input?
dataTools.statisticsFaq1A: Theoretically unlimited, but we recommend under 100,000 data points to avoid browser lag.
dataTools.statisticsFaq2Q: Are empty lines and non-numbers ignored?
dataTools.statisticsFaq2A: Yes. Empty lines, spaces, and text that cannot be parsed as numbers are automatically ignored.
dataTools.statisticsFaq3Q: How many decimal places are shown?
dataTools.statisticsFaq3A: Results are displayed to 4 decimal places for sufficient precision while maintaining readability.
